hero.label: Made for Rilla
hero.headline: I want to build Rilla's SDR motion from the ground up.
hero.subheadline: Sebastian Jimenez's philosophy of finding the right market over chasing features resonates with how I approach sales: listen first, execute second. Rilla is solving a real problem for field sales teams that nobody else is touching, and I want to be part of proving out that GTM playbook in NYC.

whyCompany.title: Rilla owns an untapped market in field sales intelligence
whyCompany.body: Home services and solar reps are doing deals face-to-face with zero feedback loops. Rilla's AI transcription and playbook matching directly solves that. This isn't a crowded market like general sales coaching. Sebastian doubled down on one vision instead of feature creep, and that focus is why the product actually works.

whyCompany.title: Entry-level hiring moment with real GTM leverage
whyCompany.body: Rilla is hiring 2026 graduates specifically to build top-of-funnel motion in NYC. This is a foundational role, which means I'm not just executing playbook. I'm helping define how Rilla acquires its first wave of field sales customers at scale. That's the kind of leverage every early SDR should want.

whyCompany.title: My BDR work translates directly to this SDR role
whyCompany.body: At Copygen.ai, I sourced and qualified 15-20 paying clients cold. I built LinkedIn sequences, managed HubSpot cadences, and handled objections real-time. Rilla's SDRs do the same thing but for a product with higher stakes (coaching managers, improving sales reps). I've already done the hard part. Now I want to do it for a product I believe in.

quote: I want to prove that a 21-year-old who knows how to prospect can help Rilla own the field sales intelligence market before anyone else realizes it's there.
